creator: n1nja@earthlink.net
text:
System:  Dell XPS 600.  3.2 Ghz dual-core Intel Pentium-D.

Upgrading from 6.2 p9 to 7.0 revealed a possible regression in 6.3 and 7.0 for the NVidia C19 / ck804 (nForce4) chipset.  On reboot into the 7.0 kernel, the system was unable to find the disks and failed just after the md0 mfsroot line.

The sysinstall for 6.3 and 7.0 is unable to recognize the CD\DVD and sata disk drives in the system.

The output of pciconf -lv Using a dsbsd live cd of 7.0 is shown below.  "atacontrol list" shows ATA channel 0 thru 5; each one has master and slave with 'no device present'.

Other information is hard to gather since I have watchdog timeouts for the ethernet cards in this system.  The pciconf -lv was hand typed but double checked for accuracy.


pciconf -lv

hostb0@pci0:0:0:0:		class=0x060000 card=0x00000000 chip=0x007110de rev=0xa3 hdr=0x00
	class		= bridge
	subclass	= HOST-PCI

	class		= memory
	subclass	= RAM

	subclass	= PCI-PCI

	class		= display
	subclass	= VGA

em0@pci0:3:3:0:	class=0x020000 card=0x13768086 chip=0x107c8086 rev=0x05 hdr=0x00
	class		= network
	subclass	= ethernet

fwohci0@pci0:3:10:0:	class=0x0c0010 card=0x01c31028 chip=0x8023104c rev=0x00 hdr=0x00
	class		= serial bus
	subclass	= FireWire

How-To-Repeat: Run the 6.3 or 7.0 sysinstall on a similar system.  Or, use a working 6.2 system and upgrade to 6.3/7.0.
